fields.label: Head to Corky's
fields.arrival_flavor: Two blocks. The sign is half-lit, the way it always is.

fields.label: Head home
fields.arrival_flavor: The walk back is two blocks. The radiator is already going.

fields.label: Head back to the Flats
fields.arrival_flavor: Your block. The radiator. The corkboard. All of it exactly where you left it.

fields.label: Head back to the Flats
fields.arrival_flavor: You peel off before the Strip gets too loud. The Flats is quieter.

fields.label: Head to Corky's
fields.arrival_flavor: The bar is two blocks from Reyes Boxing. You know the street.

fields.label: Talk to Sal directly
fields.arrival_flavor: You take a stool at the bar. He sets a glass of water in front of you without being asked.

fields.label: Approach Sal
fields.arrival_flavor: You move to the bar. He's already looking at you.

fields.label: Tell him wanting things doesn't change how they are
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice level. This isn't personal. That's almost worse.

fields.label: Let it land
fields.arrival_flavor: His shoulders drop. He nods, once, at the bar top. The dealers can come back.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He meets your eyes. Says he's not going to do it. Says it like a man who has already accepted the consequences.

fields.label: Give him a moment to do the math
fields.arrival_flavor: He looks at the shelf. Then at you. Then he tells you the dealers can come back. He doesn't say anything else.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He tells you his finances are none of your business. Tells you to get out. His voice is steady. He means it.

fields.label: Help him build a counter-offer Dutch might actually accept
fields.arrival_flavor: You think out loud. He listens. It takes twenty minutes and a second glass of water.

fields.label: Report back to Morris
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris is quiet for longer than you'd like. Then he says he'll pass it on. He doesn't say Dutch will accept it.

fields.label: Report back to Morris
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris says acceptable. That's the word — acceptable. Like something being checked off a list.

fields.label: Report back to Morris
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris says satisfied. He doesn't ask how you got there.

fields.label: Report back to Morris
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris listens. Then he says Dutch will handle it himself. You're not sure if that's better or worse for Sal.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch knows you get results. Morris said acceptable. You take it.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Sal's holding out. Dutch will handle it. You hope he knows what that costs.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: The dealers are back in on Thursday. You drove past on Wednesday.

fields.label: Head to Crane Street
fields.arrival_flavor: Early morning in the Flats. The street cleaners haven't been through yet.

fields.label: Move
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep to the main streets. Easier to look like you belong.

fields.label: Keep walking
fields.arrival_flavor: You don't break stride. If they're going to move, make them do it first.

fields.label: Deliver the package
fields.arrival_flavor: Third floor. The door opens before you knock. These people have been waiting.

fields.label: Tell Morris everything — Darius, the Kings, all of it
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris goes quiet for a moment. Then he says stay by your phone.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch's name is on the other end of a lost package. That's where you are now.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Darius said tell Dutch he says hello. Dutch now knows. Whatever that starts, it started before you got involved.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch got his package. What he doesn't know won't hurt you. Probably.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch is owed a good job. The next one has to count.

fields.label: Answer it
fields.arrival_flavor: You pick up. Neither of you says anything for a moment.

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up before you can say anything else. Classic.

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up. You sit with it for a minute before you move.

fields.label: Reason with him, tell him to back off your sister
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice low. Make sure he has to lean in to hear you.

fields.label: Let it land
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts behind his eyes. He nods, once, at the pavement. Steps back.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Some people need a different kind of convincing.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: The walk back is quiet. That's fine.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Let the adrenaline settle before you're somewhere with good lighting.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You hang your jacket up. First time in three years that felt like something normal.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You ice your ribs over the sink. The city keeps going. So do you.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Nobody should see you like this.

fields.label: Head to Presto's
fields.arrival_flavor: The laundromat is two blocks east. You can see the sign from the corner.

fields.label: Head for the lockup on Decker
fields.arrival_flavor: The package is heavier than it looks. You don't shake it.

fields.label: Take the back streets
fields.arrival_flavor: Marsh Lane is quiet. Delivery drivers and a woman walking a dog.

fields.label: Keep moving
fields.arrival_flavor: The lot opens up onto Decker south of the patrol. Clean.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: You're moving too fast. You come out on the wrong side.

fields.label: Finish the walk
fields.arrival_flavor: The lockup is exactly where Morris said it would be.

fields.label: Keep walking
fields.arrival_flavor: Package still arrives. So does the report.

fields.label: Drop it at the lockup
fields.arrival_flavor: Unit twelve. You leave the package inside and don't look back.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch knows your name. You sit with that for a minute.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: A hundred and fifty. You made the math easy for them.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You almost had it. Almost is Dutch's least favorite word. Probably.

fields.label: Take the package
fields.arrival_flavor: You pick it up. It feels heavier now that it’s yours.

fields.label: I’m passing through
fields.arrival_flavor: He watches how you say it more than what you say.

fields.label: Head to the Strip
fields.arrival_flavor: The block lets you leave.

fields.label: Play along
fields.arrival_flavor: You nod like this makes sense.

fields.label: Move to the next address
fields.arrival_flavor: The crowd absorbs you immediately. That’s the whole design.

fields.label: Keep going
fields.arrival_flavor: You don’t get paid to fix it.

fields.label: Then it’s a good thing I’m not keeping it
fields.arrival_flavor: He considers that like it might be the right answer.

fields.label: Finish the delivery
fields.arrival_flavor: Whatever this is, you’re already in it.

fields.label: Leave
fields.arrival_flavor: You’re already outside before it registers.

fields.label: Keep moving
fields.arrival_flavor: Motion is safety.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: The corkboard hasn’t changed. You have.

fields.label: Continue
fields.arrival_flavor: The timing still doesn’t sit right.

fields.label: Not keeping it
fields.arrival_flavor: That answer works.

fields.label: Finish it
fields.arrival_flavor: You’re committed now.

fields.label: Find out who's crossing the line
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods once and walks. You're already thinking about the corner.

fields.label: Follow the route
fields.arrival_flavor: The runner moves like someone who thinks nobody's watching. You stay back.

fields.label: Get closer
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance without being made. Close enough to see the face.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He clocks you at twenty feet. The lockup door closes. The runner is already gone.

fields.label: Work out what to do with it
fields.arrival_flavor: You watch the lockup door for a while. Then you move.

fields.label: Move on the lockup
fields.arrival_flavor: You know the address. That's enough to start.

fields.label: Tell him exactly what this is
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice level. Give him the shape of it before he has time to react.

fields.label: Let it land
fields.arrival_flavor: Something moves across his face. He looks at the lockup door and then back at you.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He's nineteen and scared and scared nineteen-year-olds make bad decisions. He reaches for his jacket again.

fields.label: Collect and go
fields.arrival_flavor: The contact pays without asking questions. You don't offer answers.

fields.label: Finish it
fields.arrival_flavor: No more talking.

fields.label: Move on him
fields.arrival_flavor: Four walls and a concrete floor. You account for the space.

fields.label: Get paid and go
fields.arrival_flavor: He pays. The way he doesn't ask how is its own kind of answer.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: The boundary holds. Nobody marks the occasion.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: You think about what the contact said for most of the walk back. Almost in passing.

fields.label: Walk it off
fields.arrival_flavor: The cold war gets a little warmer. Nothing you can do about it now.

fields.label: Ask Sal what he knows
fields.arrival_flavor: Sal puts the glass down. Thinks about it.

fields.label: Go to Crane Street
fields.arrival_flavor: Six blocks. You know the street. You've never had a reason to go there before.

fields.label: Tell him you know about the debt
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts in his expression. The chain comes off.

fields.label: Find the collectors
fields.arrival_flavor: He gives you the block. Doesn't ask what you're going to do.

fields.label: Talk them off it
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your hands visible. Start talking.

fields.label: Let it land
fields.arrival_flavor: The taller one looks at his partner. Something passes between them.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: The shorter one takes a step forward. That's the answer.

fields.label: Go back to Danny
fields.arrival_flavor: Crane Street is quiet. Same curtain in the window.

fields.label: Handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: No more talking.

fields.label: Move on them
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he finishes the sentence.

fields.label: Go back to Danny
fields.arrival_flavor: Crane Street is quiet. Same curtain in the window.

fields.label: Pay off the debt yourself
fields.arrival_flavor: You pull the cash out. He looks at it for a long moment.

fields.label: Stay for a drink
fields.arrival_flavor: Thursday. Danny comes in at six. Sal doesn't say anything. Pours the bitter without being asked.

fields.label: Drink it
fields.arrival_flavor: Danny doesn't come back on Tuesday. Or Thursday. You stop counting after a while.

fields.label: Sit with it
fields.arrival_flavor: Danny doesn't come back. Sal keeps his Tuesday and Thursday glasses clean anyway.

fields.label: Watch it a while longer
fields.arrival_flavor: You find a spot at the bus stop nobody uses as a bus stop and you watch.

fields.label: That's enough
fields.arrival_flavor: You know the layout. You know the timing. Time to move.

fields.label: Walk in like you belong
fields.arrival_flavor: You push the door open. The guy behind the counter doesn't look up.

fields.label: Give him something to look at
fields.arrival_flavor: He turns to check the shelf behind him. You move.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He clocks you before you're halfway there. His hand goes under the counter.

fields.label: Keep moving
fields.arrival_flavor: The back room door swings shut behind you.

fields.label: Rush him
fields.arrival_flavor: No other option. You go over the counter.

fields.label: Move on him
fields.arrival_flavor: You go in before he finishes the thought.

fields.label: Keep moving
fields.arrival_flavor: The door swings shut behind you without a sound.

fields.label: Tell him to forget he saw you
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods. Doesn't say anything. You don't know if that means anything.

fields.label: Walk out the front, don't run
fields.arrival_flavor: You push through the door at a pace that says you have somewhere to be.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: The noodle place is still open. You don't stop.

fields.label: Take the long way back
fields.arrival_flavor: You change direction twice more before you stop.

fields.label: Walk it off
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Force of habit.

fields.label: Take the drive
fields.arrival_flavor: Sal slides it across. Doesn't say thank you. Doesn't need to.

fields.label: Start asking around
fields.arrival_flavor: You know a few people who know a few people. You start there.

fields.label: Follow the thread
fields.arrival_flavor: The cousin knows something. You can tell by the way he doesn't quite meet your eyes.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Someone got there first. The cousin's already been asked and already said too much.

fields.label: Let him talk
fields.arrival_flavor: He takes a breath. Starts at the beginning.

fields.label: Decide what to do with the drive
fields.arrival_flavor: You still have it. That's all you have.

fields.label: Decide what to do
fields.arrival_flavor: He waits. Jacket still on. Ready to move the second you tell him which direction.

fields.label: Destroy the drive and send Fenn running
fields.arrival_flavor: You tell Fenn to go. He goes. You put the drive on the concrete floor and stand on it.

fields.label: Hand it to the Blues
fields.arrival_flavor: You make a call. The plainclothes officer picks up on the first ring.

fields.label: Stay for a drink
fields.arrival_flavor: Nobody comes asking about Fenn again. After a while you stop expecting them to.

fields.label: Back to the bar
fields.arrival_flavor: Sal's wiping down glasses. He doesn't look up when you come in.

fields.label: Back to the bar
fields.arrival_flavor: The envelope sits in your jacket the whole walk back. You don't count it until you're inside.

fields.label: Go back
fields.arrival_flavor: Sal's behind the bar. Same as always. Almost.

fields.label: Take the job
fields.arrival_flavor: The line goes dead before you can change your mind.

fields.label: Find the car
fields.arrival_flavor: She doesn't say good luck. You get the impression she doesn't believe in it.

fields.label: Talk it out of him
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice low. Put one hand on the roof of the car.

fields.label: Wait him out
fields.arrival_flavor: He looks at the steering wheel for a long moment. Then he talks.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He shakes his head. Reaches for his phone.

fields.label: Make a decision
fields.arrival_flavor: He's watching you through the window. Waiting.

fields.label: Pull him out
fields.arrival_flavor: You get the door open. He comes out with his hands up.

fields.label: Zip tie him to the steering wheel and walk
fields.arrival_flavor: He doesn't fight it. You make it tight enough to mean it.

fields.label: Move on him
fields.arrival_flavor: The loading bay is tight. You account for the car.

fields.label: Move
fields.arrival_flavor: You don't run. You walk like you have somewhere to be, which you do.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: The grey sedan is still in the loading bay. It'll be someone else's problem by morning.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: You got paid. The job isn't done. Those two things are going to have to coexist.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: Next time keep it cleaner. You're already thinking about what that means.

fields.label: Walk it off
fields.arrival_flavor: The burner number rings out. You don't leave a message.

fields.label: Find Calvin
fields.arrival_flavor: Deon nods. Gives you what he has. It isn't much.

fields.label: Start with the girlfriend
fields.arrival_flavor: North Westside. Buildings close together, fire escapes loaded with plants.

fields.label: Ask about the favor
fields.arrival_flavor: She thinks about it. Pulls the phone back out and puts it away again.

fields.label: Find Calvin
fields.arrival_flavor: The cousin's card game. That's where you start.

fields.label: Pull the thread
fields.arrival_flavor: The cousin's name comes up twice in the same conversation from two different people.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: The cousin's been telling people Calvin's out of town. Calvin is not out of town.

fields.label: Go in
fields.arrival_flavor: The door swings open. Calvin looks at you like he's been expecting someone, just not you.

fields.label: Tell Deon what you know
fields.arrival_flavor: Deon listens. Then he picks up his phone.

fields.label: Go in
fields.arrival_flavor: The basement door is unlocked. Calvin is inside with the lights off.

fields.label: Tell him to come back to the crew
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods slowly. Stands up. Puts his jacket on like he's been waiting for permission.

fields.label: Decide what to do with it
fields.arrival_flavor: Calvin's already standing. Jacket on. Waiting for you to tell him which direction.

fields.label: Tell Calvin to say he saw nothing, send him back clean
fields.arrival_flavor: Calvin nods. He looks relieved and guilty at the same time. You know the feeling.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: Calvin's back. The crew moves on. The corner boy is still on Crane Street.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: Deon's already on the phone before you reach the end of the block.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: The corner boy isn't on Crane Street anymore. Nobody says why.

fields.label: Back to the corner
fields.arrival_flavor: You had it. You sat on it. Now it's just something that happened.

fields.label: Head down to the Strip
fields.arrival_flavor: The Strip picks up where the Westside leaves off. Different money, same business.

fields.label: Cross into the Westside
fields.arrival_flavor: The street changes at the intersection. You feel it before you see it.

fields.label: Look around first
fields.arrival_flavor: You take a table near the window. Order nothing. Watch.

fields.label: Ask him about the envelopes on the shelf
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts in his expression. He wasn't expecting that angle.

fields.label: Tell him it doesn't matter. The dealers are coming back.
fields.arrival_flavor: The warmth goes out of the room. He picks up the cloth and goes back to the bar.

fields.label: Stop and let them come to you
fields.arrival_flavor: You put your back to the nearest wall. Choose your ground. They come off the car.

fields.label: Keep it simple. Package delivered, ran into some trouble, handled it.
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris says good work. Three hundred by end of day. You pocket your phone and go.

fields.label: Let it ring out
fields.arrival_flavor: It goes to voicemail. Twenty seconds later, a text. I need help. Please. Then a second text, four minutes after the first — Please answer, its an emergency. You decide to call her.

fields.label: Ask her why she called you specifically
fields.arrival_flavor: A pause. Then — "Because you owe me about a thousand of them, that's why."

fields.label: Don't waste words
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he finishes the question.

fields.label: Keep walking — straight down Decker
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your pace even. Don't look at the car.

fields.label: Then it’s a good day for something new
fields.arrival_flavor: That gets a look. Not a good one. Not a bad one either.

fields.label: Seems like I’m exactly on time
fields.arrival_flavor: He smiles like you told a joke he didn’t like.

fields.label: Make the point physically
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he finishes reading you.

fields.label: Ask if anyone's been coming around
fields.arrival_flavor: He looks at you for a long moment. Then the chain comes off.

fields.label: Make it physical
fields.arrival_flavor: The shorter one sees it coming. The taller one doesn't.

fields.label: Tell him to lie low for a while
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods. Says he understands. He's been doing that for two weeks already.

fields.label: You've seen enough
fields.arrival_flavor: You've been clocking this place for three days. That's enough.

fields.label: Go in hard
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he has time to think about it.

fields.label: Take his phone first
fields.arrival_flavor: He hands it over without being asked twice. Smart kid.

fields.label: Walk out the front, don't run
fields.arrival_flavor: You push through the door. The car doesn't move.

fields.label: Hand it to the Blues
fields.arrival_flavor: You tell Fenn to find somewhere to be. Then you make a call.

fields.label: Hand it to the Bucco associate
fields.arrival_flavor: The associate is easy to find. He's been waiting somewhere close. You had a feeling.

fields.label: Pull him out of the car
fields.arrival_flavor: You get the door open before he decides whether to lock it.

fields.label: Tell him to drive and not come back
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods. Starts the car. You watch him pull out of the loading bay.

fields.label: Ask if he said anything else
fields.arrival_flavor: She goes back through it carefully, the way people do when they wish they'd paid more attention.

fields.label: Ask him who the man in the suit was
fields.arrival_flavor: He thinks about it. Describes him carefully, the way scared people remember things.

fields.label: Tell Deon exactly what Calvin saw
fields.arrival_flavor: Deon picks up on the first ring. You tell him to meet you.

fields.label: Cross into the Westside
fields.arrival_flavor: Different block, different rules. You clock the corners before you move.

fields.label: Head down to the Strip
fields.arrival_flavor: The Strip is louder than the Flats. It always is.

fields.label: Ask him what he actually needs to make this work
fields.arrival_flavor: He looks at you for a long moment. Deciding whether you mean it.

fields.label: Take the side alley
fields.arrival_flavor: The alley is empty. The fire exit is exactly where you clocked it.

fields.label: Zip tie him to the shelf
fields.arrival_flavor: He doesn't fight it. You make it tight enough to mean it, loose enough to matter.

fields.label: Back out the side door
fields.arrival_flavor: The alley is still empty. You knew it would be.

fields.label: Hand it to the Bucco associate
fields.arrival_flavor: You tell Fenn you'll buy him an hour. You're not sure you mean it.

fields.label: Destroy it and walk away
fields.arrival_flavor: You put the drive on the pavement and stand on it. Then you walk.

fields.label: Take it to the Kings council directly
fields.arrival_flavor: You tell Calvin to sit tight one more hour. Then you make a call.

fields.label: Head down to the Strip
fields.arrival_flavor: The Strip is louder than the Flats. It always is.

fields.label: Keep the information yourself, send Calvin back with nothing
fields.arrival_flavor: Calvin goes. You sit in the basement for a while and think about what you're holding.
